ops.title: wall_of_text panel flaky negatives (nb04/nb05/nb06): tighten the prompt's exception boundaries
ops.description:
Rubric-panel flaky negatives in the show pack's wall_of_text PostToolUse hook (plugins/show/hooks/wall_of_text.py). Filed from the round-4 sign-off, 2026-07-05.

## Evidence (measured, live-LLM, fresh session_id per run)

- nb04 "option dump alongside an AskUserQuestion call": 0/14 baseline → 1/10 at v0.1.2.
- nb05 "user asked for plain text": fires ~3/4 at window=6 and 2/10 at window="turn"; gate-passes at both windows, so the flake is prompt-side, not gate-side.
- nb06 "two trivial options": 1/6 → 1/2.
- Flaky fires cite option-list reasons and never the v0.1.2 design carve-out — the carve-out holds; the exception boundaries (already-structured, plain-text-requested, trivially-small) are what leak.

Panel: see the note tagged `round5-panel` in this repo (attachment `show-rubric-panel.tar.gz`; attachment content moves on `cc-notes sync`, not plain fetch). 16+ cases f01–f10 / nb01–nb10, manifest.json, per-case out/err logs.

## Plan

1. Restore the panel from the attachment; re-baseline nb04/nb05/nb06 at v0.1.3, N≥10 each, fresh session_id per run — round 4's nb05 verdict flipped with sample size, so never trust N<10.
2. Per case decide tighten-prompt vs adjust-rubric-expectation:
   - nb04: the surface-already-carried exception (an AskUserQuestion accompanied the dump). Consider whether 1/10 tail noise is acceptable and the rubric optimistic.
   - nb05: strengthen the plain-text-request exception — likely a contrastive example (v0.1.2 added one for f08; same pattern).
   - nb06: the two-trivial-options smallness boundary.
3. At most two prompt iterations. Each iteration: N≥10 on the changed boundary plus a full positive sweep — f01–f10 must keep firing. Never weaken positives to quiet negatives.
4. Patch-bump `plugins/show/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (plugin.json drives pack re-fetch, not capt-hook.toml), push.

## Environment state (verified 2026-07-05)

show pack 0.1.3 (window="turn" + f08 contrastive example), capt-hook 8.5.0 on PyPI, spawnllm 0.5.5.

ops.text: nb04, nb05, nb06 each fire ≤1/10 on an N≥10 fresh-session baseline at the shipped version
Full positive sweep f01–f10 still fires after every prompt change
show pack version patch-bumped in plugins/show/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and pushed
